name: CRUD/history
description: History (GET /_history, /{type}/_history, /{type}/{id}/_history) conformance tests: non-empty system/type/instance Bundles, correct default and explicit sort order, well-formed entry.response.status, and _summary=count/_since/_before/_sort parameter handling (from HistoryTests).

variable.name: histAId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient used for the instance-history/sort-order tests

variable.name: histStatusId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the dedicated Patient used for the response.status test

operation.description: Create the Patient used for the instance-history and sort-order tests

test.name: system-level history returns a non-empty bundle
test.description: GET /_history must return HTTP 200 and a Bundle with at least one entry, since resources exist on the server.

assert.description: Bundle must contain at least one entry

test.name: type-level history returns a non-empty bundle
test.description: GET /Patient/_history must return HTTP 200 and a Bundle with at least one entry, since the Patient created in setup exists.

assert.description: Bundle must contain at least one entry

test.name: instance history reflects create then update, newest first by default
test.description: After updating the Patient created in setup, GET /Patient/{id}/_history must return at least two entries with the newest version first (default order).

operation.description: Read back the Patient to use as the round-tripped update body
Round-trip update the Patient to produce a second version

assert.description: Update must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: GET instance-level history for the Patient

assert.description: Must contain at least two entries (the create and the update)
Default order must be newest first (entry[0] lastUpdated >= entry[1] lastUpdated)

test.name: instance history with explicit ascending sort returns oldest first
test.description: GET /Patient/{id}/_history?_sort=_lastUpdated on the (now two-version) Patient must return entries oldest first.

operation.description: GET instance history sorted ascending by _lastUpdated

assert.description: Ascending order must hold (entry[0] lastUpdated <= entry[1] lastUpdated)

test.name: instance history with explicit descending sort matches default order
test.description: GET /Patient/{id}/_history?_sort=-_lastUpdated on the (now two-version) Patient must return entries newest first, matching the default order.

operation.description: GET instance history sorted descending by _lastUpdated

assert.description: Descending order must hold (entry[0] lastUpdated >= entry[1] lastUpdated)

test.name: history entries report a well-formed response.status for every version
test.description: A dedicated Patient is created, updated, and deleted so its instance history contains three distinct operations. Every Bundle.entry.response.status must be present and well-formed (a leading 3-digit HTTP status code).

operation.description: Create the dedicated Patient for the response.status test
Read back the Patient to use as the round-tripped update body
Round-trip update the Patient to produce a second version

assert.description: Update must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Delete the Patient to produce a third history entry

assert.description: Delete must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: GET instance-level history for the dedicated Patient

assert.description: Must contain at least three entries (create, update, delete)
Every entry must report a response.status
Every entry's response.status must start with a 3-digit HTTP status code
Server extension detail: the most recent entry's response.status should reflect the delete (typically 204)

test.name: _summary=count on history returns totals without entries
test.description: GET /Patient/_history?_summary=count must return HTTP 200 and a Bundle whose total is populated but which contains no entries.

assert.description: Bundle must not contain entries when _summary=count is used

test.name: a far-past _since does not wrongly exclude existing history
test.description: GET /_history?_since=2000-01-01T00:00:00Z, a date far before any resource on the server was created, must still return the existing history (non-empty Bundle).

operation.description: GET system-level history with a far-past _since

assert.description: Bundle must contain at least one entry

test.name: a far-future _before returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: GET /_history?_before=2999-01-01T00:00:00Z, a date far in the future, must be rejected with 400 Bad Request since history cannot be requested before a time that hasn't happened yet. This is a server-side validation choice rather than a base FHIR R4 mandate, so it is warningOnly.

operation.description: GET system-level history with a far-future _before

assert.description: Server validation: expect 400 Bad Request for a future _before value

test.name: _sort=_id on history is unsupported and returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: GET /Patient/_history?_sort=_id must be rejected with 400 Bad Request since history only supports sorting by _lastUpdated. This is a server-side validation choice rather than a base FHIR R4 mandate, so it is warningOnly.

operation.description: GET type-level history with an unsupported _sort value

assert.description: Server validation: expect 400 Bad Request for an unsupported _sort value

operation.description: Teardown: delete the response.status test Patient (already deleted by the test, best effort)
